questions.question: What does `float: left` do to an element?
choices: Moves the element to the left, allowing content to wrap around its right side
Pins the element to the left side of the viewport
Aligns text within the element to the left
questions.explanation: float: left takes the element out of normal flow and positions it to the left of its container, with surrounding content wrapping around its right side.

questions.question: What problem does the clearfix technique solve?
choices: Preventing text from wrapping around floated elements
A parent container collapsing to zero height when all children are floated
Removing the default margin on floated elements
questions.explanation: When all children are floated, the parent loses its height. The clearfix technique forces the parent to contain its floated children.

questions.question: Which property is used to prevent an element from appearing next to a floated element?
questions.explanation: The clear property (with values left, right, or both) forces an element to move below any preceding floated elements.

questions.question: Which value of `clear` prevents the element from sitting beside floats on either side?
questions.explanation: clear: both clears floats on both the left and right sides, ensuring the element starts below any preceding floated elements.

questions.question: Setting `float: ___` removes a float and returns the element to normal document flow.
questions.explanation: float: none is the default value that keeps an element in normal flow without floating.

questions.question: The ___ property specifies whether an element should be moved below preceding floated elements.
questions.explanation: The clear property controls whether an element is moved down to clear past left, right, or both floats.
